title: platform-user — helper functions
description: Two helper facades exposed for internal tooling that reaches PlatformUser + on-call state without going through the full HTTP layer (Artisan commands, listeners, notifications composing enrichment text). Every helper delegates to a service binding under the `platform-user` container namespace; helpers are the ergonomic surface.

helpers.purpose: Resolve the current platform_admin-guard PlatformUser (when called without args) OR resolve a specific PlatformUser by ULID (`plu_...` or `idn_...`). Null when the caller is not on the platform_admin guard OR the id does not resolve.
behaviours: platform_user() when authenticated on the platform_admin guard → returns Auth::guard('platform_admin')->user() cast to PlatformUser
platform_user() when authenticated on the sanctum guard → returns null (guard boundary; never cross)
platform_user('idn_01H...') → resolves the PlatformUser via UNIQUE(identity_id)
platform_user('idn_01H...') when the identity has no linked PlatformUser → returns null (an identity may be a tenant User only)

helpers.purpose: Resolve the currently-on-call PlatformUser (platform-wide when department is null; scoped when set). Returns an OnCallShift value object with platform_user, shift_end_at, source ('internal' | 'pagerduty'). Null when no shift is active OR the OnCallResolver is disabled.
behaviours: on_call_now() during business hours with a live rotation → returns the active shift
on_call_now('security') → scoped to the security department's rotation
on_call_now() during a resolver timeout → returns null (never throws; falls back to Slack broadcast at the caller layer)

notes: There is deliberately NO helper equivalent for `platform_user_can()` — permission checks go through the standard Gate::allows() with a PlatformUser instance to preserve the audit trail on the meta-audit rows.
The `platform_user()` helper is available in ALL contexts (Artisan, HTTP, queue workers). In a queue worker, the caller must pass an explicit id — there is no request-context guard.

The `on_call_now()` helper is safe to call from unauthenticated contexts (e.g. an incident-response webhook) because it does not require an actor — it only reads the resolver. When enabled + the backend is PagerDuty, the caller pays a ~200ms round-trip; the value is cached per the caches.json policy.
